task_title: Integrate AI OS governance into Tiem Nuoc Nho v5
context: Project PRJ-004 was registered in registry.json but had no governance files. AI OS could validate workspace via gatekeeper but Claude Code had no project-level rules or workflows to follow.

<AI_OS_ROOT>\\registry.json: Updated all paths from LongLeo machine to current machine layout
Created: project constitution with boot sequence, identity, key decisions, pending features
<AI_OS_ROOT>\\projects\\PRJ-004\\workflo: Created: session init workflow (gatekeeper, TS check, blackboard check)
Created: session close workflow (TS final check, blackboard update, soul backup)
Created: POS feature development SOP (5 phases + code patterns)
D:\\Tiem_Nuoc_Nho_v5\\.clauderules: Created: project-specific behavioral rules for Claude Code

architecture_decision.decision: AI OS governance files live centrally in <AI_OS_ROOT>\projects\PRJ-004\ — NOT in the project folder
architecture_decision.rationale: Keeps source code clean. AI OS manages all governance. Only .clauderules must stay in workspace root (Claude Code technical requirement).
architecture_decision.exception: .clauderules MUST be in workspace root — Claude Code reads it automatically from there

notes: Full AI OS integration complete. Gatekeeper GRANT confirmed. From now on all agents working on PRJ-004 follow governance defined in AI OS central, not the project folder.
patterns_discovered: AI OS project config should live in AI OS central (projects/PRJ-XXX/), NOT in the project workspace
Only .clauderules must stay at workspace root — this is a Claude Code hard requirement
registry.json is the single source of truth for workspace-to-config mapping
